Karnapmveya is synonymous with Karna prnvaranarazntiontd in
the Ramayana (IV. 40.26) according to Kern.
Puraiava is the son of a Brahmana by a ^tidra woman. Here
it is identified with Persia. In chapter LXXXI-2, 6 this country is
said to be the producer of excellent pearls..
The Sudras are associated with the Abhiras and are located
in West Rajputana near Vina^ana, according to the Mahabharata
(II. 32.10; IX.37.1). Alexander's historians call them Sodrai who
lived between the Indus and the Sutlej above the junction of the
five rivers near Mithankot and south of the district of Multan.
They had their capital on the Indus.
The Barbaras are located in the region about the port of Bar-
baricum situated on the middle mouth of the indus, according to the
Periplus. The Mnrkandeyapurvna (LVII. 39) places the Barbara
country in the Sindhu valley.
